foreground.h(3) Library Functions Manual foreground.h(3)

NAME

foreground.h

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * image_print (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_256color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t *image, bool use_background, const char *palette)

Function Documentation

char * image_print (const image_t * p, const char * palette)

Definition at line 27 of file foreground.c.

27                                                          {
28   if (!p || !palette) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
30     return NULL;
31   }
32   if (!p->pixels) {
33     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p->pixels is NULL");
34     return NULL;
35   }
36 
37   const int h = p->h;
38   const int w = p->w;
39 
40   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
46   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
47   if (!utf8_cache) {
48     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar rendering");
49     return NULL;
50   }
51 
52   // Character index ramp is now part of UTF-8 cache - no separate cache needed
53 
54   // Need space for h rows with UTF-8 characters, plus h-1 newlines, plus null terminator
55   const size_t max_char_bytes = 4; // Max UTF-8 character size
56 
57   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
58 
59   // Use outbuf_t for efficient UTF-8 RLE emission (same as SIMD renderers)
60   outbuf_t ob = {0};
61 
62   // Calculate buffer size with overflow checking
63   size_t w_times_bytes;
64   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)w, max_char_bytes, &w_times_bytes) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
65     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width too large for UTF-8 encoding");
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   size_t w_times_bytes_plus_one;
70   if (checked_size_add(w_times_bytes, 1, &w_times_bytes_plus_one) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width * bytes + 1 overflow");
72     return NULL;
73   }
74 
75   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, w_times_bytes_plus_one, &ob.cap) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
76     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: height * (width * bytes + 1) overflow");
77     return NULL;
78   }
79 
80   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
81   if (!ob.buf) {
82     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate output buffer for scalar rendering");
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   // Process pixels with UTF-8 RLE emission (same approach as SIMD)
87   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
88     const int row_offset = y * w;
89 
90     for (int x = 0; x < w;) {
91       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
92       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
93       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
94 
95       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
96       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
97       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
98       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
99 
100       // Use same 64-entry cache as SIMD for consistency
101       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache64[luma_idx];
102 
103       // Find run length for same character (RLE optimization)
104       int j = x + 1;
105       while (j < w) {
106         const rgb_pixel_t next_pixel = pix[row_offset + j];
107         const int next_luminance = (77 * next_pixel.r + 150 * next_pixel.g + 29 * next_pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
108         uint8_t next_safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(next_luminance);
109         uint8_t next_luma_idx = (uint8_t)(next_safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
110         uint8_t next_char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[next_luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
111         if (next_char_idx != char_idx)
112           break;
113         j++;
114       }
115       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
116 
117       // Emit UTF-8 character with RLE (same as SIMD)
118       ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
119       if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
120         emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
121       } else {
122         for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; k++) {
123           ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
124         }
125       }
126       x = j;
127     }
128 
129     // Add newline between rows (except last row)
130     if (y != h - 1) {
131       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
132     }
133   }
134 
135   ob_term(&ob);
136   return ob.buf;
137 }








char
  * image_print_16color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 463 of file foreground.c.


463                                                                      {
464   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
465     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
466     return NULL;
467   }
468 
469   int h = image->h;
470   int w = image->w;
471 
472   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
473     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
474     return NULL;
475   }
476 
477   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
478   ansi_fast_init_16color();
479 
480   // Calculate buffer size (smaller than 256-color due to shorter ANSI sequences)
481   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
482   // Calculate with overflow checking
483   size_t h_times_w;
484   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
485     return NULL;
486   }
487 
488   size_t h_times_w_times_12;
489   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 12u, &h_times_w_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
490     return NULL;
491   }
492 
493   size_t buffer_size;
494   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
495     return NULL;
496   }
497 
498   char *buffer;
499   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
500 
501   char *ptr = buffer;
502   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
503 
504   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
505   if (!utf8_cache) {
506     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color rendering");
507     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
508     return NULL;
509   }
510 
511   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
512     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
513       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
514 
515       // Convert RGB to 16-color index and generate ANSI sequence
516       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
517       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
518 
519       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
520       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
521 
522       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
523       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
524       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
525       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
526 
527       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
528       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
529       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
530 
531       if (char_info) {
532         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
533         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
534           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
535         }
536       } else {
537         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
538         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
539         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
540         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
541       }
542     }
543 
544     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
545     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
546     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
547     if (y < h - 1) {
548       *ptr++ = '\n';
549     }
550   }
551 
552   *ptr = '\0';
553   return buffer;
554 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 580 of file foreground.c.


580                                                                               {
581   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
582     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
583     return NULL;
584   }
585 
586   int h = image->h;
587   int w = image->w;
588 
589   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
590     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
591     return NULL;
592   }
593 
594   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
595   ansi_fast_init_16color();
596 
597   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
598   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
599   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
600   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
601 
602   // Calculate buffer size (same as non-dithered version)
603   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
604   // Calculate with overflow checking
605   size_t h_times_w2;
606   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w2) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
607     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
608     return NULL;
609   }
610 
611   size_t h_times_w2_times_12;
612   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w2, 12u, &h_times_w2_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
613     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
614     return NULL;
615   }
616 
617   size_t buffer_size;
618   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w2_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
619     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
620     return NULL;
621   }
622 
623   char *buffer;
624   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
625 
626   char *ptr = buffer;
627   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
628 
629   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
630   if (!utf8_cache) {
631     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered rendering");
632     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
633     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
634     return NULL;
635   }
636 
637   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
638     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
639       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
640 
641       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
642       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
643       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
644 
645       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
646       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
647 
648       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
649       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
650       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
651       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
652 
653       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
654       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
655       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
656 
657       if (char_info) {
658         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
659         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
660           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
661         }
662       } else {
663         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
664         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
665         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
666         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
667       }
668     }
669 
670     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
671     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
672     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
673     if (y < h - 1) {
674       *ptr++ = '\n';
675     }
676   }
677 
678   *ptr = '\0';
679   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
680   return buffer;
681 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t * image,
  bool use_background, const char * palette)


Definition at line 684 of file foreground.c.


684                                                                                                                    {
685   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
686     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
687     return NULL;
688   }
689 
690   int h = image->h;
691   int w = image->w;
692 
693   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
694     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered_with_background: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
695     return NULL;
696   }
697 
698   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
699   ansi_fast_init_16color();
700 
701   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
702   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
703   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
704   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
705 
706   // Calculate buffer size (larger for background mode due to more ANSI sequences)
707   size_t buffer_size =
708       (size_t)h * (size_t)w * (use_background ? 24 : 12) + (size_t)h; // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
709   char *buffer;
710   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
711 
712   char *ptr = buffer;
713   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
714 
715   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
716   if (!utf8_cache) {
717     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered bg rendering");
718     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
719     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
720     return NULL;
721   }
722 
723   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
724     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
725       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
726 
727       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
728       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
729 
730       if (use_background) {
731         // Background mode: use contrasting foreground on background color
732         uint8_t bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
733         get_16color_rgb(color_index, &bg_r, &bg_g, &bg_b);
734 
735         // Calculate luminance to choose contrasting foreground
736         int bg_luminance = (bg_r * 77 + bg_g * 150 + bg_b * 29) / 256;
737         uint8_t fg_color = (bg_luminance < 127) ? 15 : 0; // White on dark, black on bright
738 
739         ptr = append_16color_bg(ptr, color_index); // Set background to pixel color
740         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, fg_color);    // Set contrasting foreground
741       } else {
742         // Foreground mode: set foreground to pixel color
743         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
744       }
745 
746       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
747       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
748 
749       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
750       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
751       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
752       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
753 
754       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
755       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
756       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
757 
758       if (char_info) {
759         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
760         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
761           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
762         }
763       } else {
764         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
765         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
766         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
767         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
768       }
769     }
770 
771     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
772     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
773     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
774     if (y < h - 1) {
775       *ptr++ = '\n';
776     }
777   }
778 
779   *ptr = '\0';
780   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
781   return buffer;
782 }










char
  * image_print_256color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 358 of file foreground.c.


358                                                                       {
359   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
360     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
361     return NULL;
362   }
363 
364   int h = image->h;
365   int w = image->w;
366 
367   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
368     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_256color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
369     return NULL;
370   }
371 
372   ansi_fast_init_256color();
373 
374   // Buffer size: 256-color ANSI codes are "\033[38;5;NNNm" (max 13 bytes) + char + reset + newline
375   size_t h_times_w;
376   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
377     return NULL;
378   }
379 
380   size_t h_times_w_times_18;
381   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 18u, &h_times_w_times_18) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
382     return NULL;
383   }
384 
385   size_t buffer_size;
386   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_18, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
387     return NULL;
388   }
389 
390   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
391   char *ptr = buffer;
392   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
393 
394   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
395   if (!utf8_cache) {
396     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 256-color rendering");
397     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
398     return NULL;
399   }
400 
401   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
402     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
403       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
404 
405       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
406       ptr = append_256color_fg(ptr, color_index);
407 
408       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
409 
410       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
411       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
412       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
413 
414       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
415       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
416 
417       if (char_info) {
418         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
419           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
420         }
421       } else {
422         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
423         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
424         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
425       }
426     }
427 
428     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
429     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
430     if (y < h - 1) {
431       *ptr++ = '\n';
432     }
433   }
434 
435   *ptr = '\0';
436   return buffer;
437 }










char
  * image_print_color (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


Converts an image to colored ASCII art with ANSI escape codes.




This function generates a string representation of an image where
    each pixel is converted to an ASCII character with ANSI color codes. The
    character is chosen based on luminance, and colors are applied using 24-bit
    RGB ANSI escape sequences.




Buffer allocation is precisely calculated to avoid waste and
    prevent overflows:


    
  
  • Each pixel: 1 ASCII char + foreground ANSI code (19 bytes max)
    • 
  
  • Background mode: adds background ANSI code (19 bytes max per pixel)
    • 
  
  • Each row: reset sequence (\033[0m = 4 bytes) + newline (except last
    row)
    • 
  
  • At the end: null terminator (1 byte)
    • 



Color modes:


    
  
  • Foreground only (default): ASCII characters with colored foreground
    • 
  
  • Background mode (opt_background_color): colored background with
      contrasting foreground (black on bright backgrounds, white on dark
      backgrounds)
    • 



ANSI escape code format:


    
  
  • Foreground: \033[38;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Background: \033[48;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Reset: \033[0m (4 bytes)
    • 



Parameters


p Pointer to image_t structure containing
  pixel data


Returns


Dynamically allocated string containing colored ASCII
  art, or NULL on error. Caller is responsible for freeing the returned
  string.


Note


The function performs overflow checks to prevent integer
  overflow when calculating buffer sizes for very large images.



Uses global opt_background_color to determine color mode.




Exits with ERROR_BUFFER if buffer overflow is detected during
    string construction (should never happen with correct calculation).




Definition at line 191 of file foreground.c.


191                                                                {
192   if (!p || !palette) {
193     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
194     return NULL;
195   }
196   if (!p->pixels) {
197     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p->pixels is NULL");
198     return NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
202   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
203   if (!utf8_cache) {
204     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar color rendering");
205     return NULL;
206   }
207 
208   const int h = p->h;
209   const int w = p->w;
210 
211   // Constants for ANSI escape codes (using exact sizes from ansi_fast.c)
212   const size_t max_fg_ansi = 19; // \033[38;2;255;255;255m
213   const size_t max_bg_ansi = 19; // \033[48;2;255;255;255m
214   const size_t reset_len = 4;    // \033[0m
215 
216   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
217   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
218 
219   // Ensure h * w won't overflow
220   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
221     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Image dimensions too large: %d x %d", h, w);
222     return NULL;
223   }
224 
225   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
226   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 1 + max_fg_ansi + max_bg_ansi; // Conservative estimate for max possible
227 
228   // Ensure total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel won't overflow
229   if (total_pixels > SIZE_MAX / bytes_per_pixel) {
230     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pixel data too large for buffer: %d x %d", h, w);
231     return NULL;
232   }
233 
234   const size_t pixel_bytes = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel;
235 
236   // Per row: newline (except last row)
237   // Final reset added once by ansi_rle_finish()
238   const size_t total_newlines = (h_sz > 0) ? (h_sz - 1) : 0;
239   const size_t final_reset = reset_len; // One reset at end (from ansi_rle_finish)
240 
241   // Final buffer size: pixel bytes + per-row newlines + final reset + null terminator
242   const size_t extra_bytes = total_newlines + final_reset + 1;
243 
244   if (pixel_bytes > SIZE_MAX - extra_bytes) {
245     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Final buffer size would overflow: %d x %d", h, w);
246     return NULL;
247   }
248 
249   const size_t lines_size = pixel_bytes + extra_bytes;
250   char *lines;
251   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
252 
253   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
254   // char *current_pos = lines;
255   // const char *buffer_end = lines + lines_size - 1; // reserve space for '\0'
256 
257   // Initialize the optimized RLE context for color sequence caching
258   // Note: This function should be called via image_print_with_capabilities() for proper per-client rendering
259   ansi_rle_context_t rle_ctx;
260   ansi_color_mode_t color_mode = ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND; // Default to foreground-only for legacy usage
261   ansi_rle_init(&rle_ctx, lines, lines_size, color_mode);
262 
263   // Process each pixel using the optimized RLE context
264   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
265     const int row_offset = y * w;
266 
267     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
268       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
269       int r = pixel.r, g = pixel.g, b = pixel.b;
270       // Standard ITU-R BT.601 luminance calculation (uses original values for accuracy)
271       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
272 
273       // Use UTF-8 character cache for proper character selection
274       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
275       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
276 
277       // For RLE, we need to pass the first byte of the UTF-8 character
278       // Note: RLE system may need updates for full UTF-8 support
279       const char ascii_char = char_info->utf8_bytes[0];
280 
281       ansi_rle_add_pixel(&rle_ctx, (uint8_t)r, (uint8_t)g, (uint8_t)b, ascii_char);
282     }
283 
284     // Add newline after each row (except the last row)
285     if (y != h - 1 && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity - 1) {
286       rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = '\n';
287     }
288   }
289 
290   ansi_rle_finish(&rle_ctx);
291 
292   return lines;
293 }












Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.8
    ascii-chat
